laws.title: Public Law 35-256 | Workers First Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: The act is an operative but uncodified tax-rate change; the source text does not identify a precise positive-law section rewrite target.

provisions.summary: Short title and definitions.
provisions.evidence: The bill may be cited as the Workers First Act of 2025 and defines the core terms.

provisions.summary: Reduces the income tax rate on American dollar income from 28 percent to 23 percent.
provisions.evidence: The source says the tax rate shall be reduced from 28% to 23%.

provisions.summary: Purpose and limitations clauses.
provisions.evidence: The text states the policy goal and limitations but does not supply a codifiable rewrite target.

provisions.summary: Immediate enactment clause.
provisions.evidence: The bill shall take effect immediately after its passage.

current_implementation.detail: The branch keeps the act as a note-level entry, but it still needs cleanup and no exact Code target is identified.

recommended_actions: Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.
Keep this as a statutory note unless a later source identifies a precise Code target.
Do not invent a codified target from the general tax-rate language.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The act is an operative but uncodified tax-rate change; the source text does not identify a precise positive-law section rewrite target.
laws.final_recommended_action: Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link. | Keep this as a statutory note unless a later source identifies a precise Code target. | Do not invent a codified target from the general tax-rate language.

laws.title: Public Law 36-256 | Commerce Dispersal Act
laws.status_basis: The act is an operative structural reorganization that transfers Commerce and Labor functions rather than expiring or being repealed.

provisions.summary: Short title and severability.
provisions.evidence: The act may be cited as the Commerce Dispersal Act and includes severability.

provisions.summary: Dissolves Commerce and Labor and transfers its functions, enforcement, and related offices.
provisions.target: Department of Commerce and Labor and related successor offices
provisions.exact_change: Transfer Commerce and Labor functions to State or Justice as specified in the act.
provisions.evidence: The source says the Department of Commerce and Labor shall be rendered inoperable and its functions transferred.

provisions.summary: Transfers the Copyright Office and Copyright Royalty Board functions to the Department of State and the D.C. district court.
provisions.target: Library of Congress / Copyright Office / Copyright Royalty Board
provisions.exact_change: Move copyright administration out of the Library of Congress and into the Department of State, with CRB jurisdiction transferred to the D.C. district court.
provisions.evidence: The source says the Copyright Office shall be transferred from the Library of Congress to the Department of State.

current_implementation.detail: The branch has a subject-matter transfer note, but it still needs to be reduced to concise transfer history and cleaned of dump/Trello baggage.

recommended_actions: Keep only a concise transfer note.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.
Do not convert the restructuring into permanent positive-law sections.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The act is an operative structural reorganization that transfers Commerce and Labor functions rather than expiring or being repealed.
laws.final_recommended_action: Keep only a concise transfer note. | Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link. | Do not convert the restructuring into permanent positive-law sections.
laws.source_evidence: active keyword `in effect` in list or label | view | https://drive.google.com/file/d/10n3NtOr_EcJP0IUYQj60PMHnGd_6X-97/view | 21f54baec9f042676779bd14992f6f2b685f6f43896e97b36afd2fb8b7e596d7
laws.exact_change: Transfer Commerce and Labor functions to State or Justice as specified in the act. | Move copyright administration out of the Library of Congress and into the Department of State, with CRB jurisdiction transferred to the D.C. district court.

laws.title: Public Law 36-257 | Maritime Operations & Policy Overhaul Act
laws.status_basis: The act is a continuing Coast Guard and maritime-policy amendment measure, not a repealed enactment.

provisions.summary: Short title, purpose, and severability.
provisions.evidence: The Act may be cited as the Maritime Operations & Policy Overhaul Act and includes severability.

provisions.summary: Amends the Streamlining and Protecting American Security Act section 6(m).
provisions.target: Streamlining and Protecting American Security Act, section 6(m)
provisions.exact_change: Add the United States Coast Guard and subordinate units to the listed heads.
provisions.evidence: Section 6(m) shall be amended to list the Coast Guard and subordinate units.

provisions.summary: Amends the Military Officer Commissioning Reform Act section 9(g).
provisions.target: Military Officer Commissioning Reform Act, section 9(g)
provisions.exact_change: Add Coast Guard Commandant attendance and related language.
provisions.evidence: Section 9(g) shall be amended to add the Coast Guard Commandant paragraph.

provisions.summary: Amends the Military Justice Reform Act of 2024 to redefine Armed Services.
provisions.target: Military Justice Reform Act of 2024, section 4(a)
provisions.exact_change: Redefine Armed Services to list the Army, Marine Corps, Air Force, Space Force, and Navy.
provisions.evidence: Section 4(a) of the Military Justice Reform Act of 2024 shall be amended to redefine Armed Services.

provisions.summary: Contingent Coast Guard assumption of duties and maritime-security coordination.
provisions.evidence: The Coast Guard may assume duties and the Commandant serves as principal maritime security adviser.

current_implementation.detail: The branch parks the measure in a maritime note, but it still needs cleanup and should remain a concise amendment/transfer history.

recommended_actions: Keep the Coast Guard amendments as concise amendment notes.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.
Do not fabricate a positive-law Code rewrite from the uncodified operational directives.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The act is a continuing Coast Guard and maritime-policy amendment measure, not a repealed enactment.
laws.final_recommended_action: Keep the Coast Guard amendments as concise amendment notes. | Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link. | Do not fabricate a positive-law Code rewrite from the uncodified operational directives.

laws.exact_change: Add the United States Coast Guard and subordinate units to the listed heads. | Add Coast Guard Commandant attendance and related language. | Redefine Armed Services to list the Army, Marine Corps, Air Force, Space Force, and Navy.

laws.title: Public Law 36-258 | National Root Beer Day Act
laws.status_basis: The holiday insertion is already effective and remains part of the federal-holiday list under 5 U.S.C. 6103(a).

provisions.summary: Short title and enactment clause.
provisions.evidence: The bill may be cited as the National Root Beer Day Act and takes effect immediately.

provisions.summary: Amends 5 U.S.C. 6103(a) by inserting National Root Beer Day after Memorial Day.
provisions.exact_change: Insert National Root Beer Day, June 17, after the Memorial Day clause in subsection (a).
provisions.evidence: The source says 5 U.S.C. 6103(a) is amended by inserting after Memorial Day, the last Monday in May.

current_implementation.detail: The 5 U.S.C. 6103(a) insertion is present and matches origin/main; only the collateral note wrapper needs cleanup.

recommended_actions: Retain the 5 U.S.C. 6103(a) insertion.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.
Keep the short-title and effective-date language only as a note.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The holiday insertion is already effective and remains part of the federal-holiday list under 5 U.S.C. 6103(a).
laws.final_recommended_action: Retain the 5 U.S.C. 6103(a) insertion. | Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link. | Keep the short-title and effective-date language only as a note.

laws.exact_change: Insert National Root Beer Day, June 17, after the Memorial Day clause in subsection (a).

laws.title: Public Law 36-260 | Fixing the District of Columbia Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: The District transition act remains operative and governs the repeal, transfer, adoption, and amendment chain for the District-related laws it names.

provisions.summary: Short title, purpose, and findings.
provisions.evidence: The act may be cited as the Fixing the District of Columbia Act of 2025 and includes extensive findings.

provisions.exact_change: Repeal the six listed laws in their entirety.
provisions.evidence: Section 4(a) says the listed laws shall be repealed in their entirety.

provisions.summary: Definitions for the municipal-government transition.
provisions.evidence: The section defines municipal government, Metropolitan Police Department, and related transition terms.

provisions.summary: Dissolves the municipal government, transfers MPD to DOJ, transfers DCFEMS to DHS, and reestablishes the D.C. Delegate.
provisions.target: District of Columbia municipal government, MPD, DCFEMS, and Delegate office
provisions.exact_change: Dissolve the municipal government and transfer MPD, DCFEMS, and the Delegate functions as specified.
provisions.evidence: The source says the municipal government is hereby dissolved and the MPD and DCFEMS are hereby transferred.

provisions.summary: Adopts several District municipal laws into federal law with specified amendments.
provisions.target: District Tort Act, DC Criminal Code, Protecting our Firefighters Act, and Fire Marshals Act
provisions.exact_change: Adopt the named municipal laws into federal law with the listed amendments.
provisions.evidence: It says those municipal laws are hereby adopted and enacted as federal law with listed amendments.

provisions.summary: Amends Public Law 22-156 to place MPD under DOJ authority and revise leadership language.
provisions.exact_change: Amend the listed subsections of Public Law 22-156 to read as specified.
provisions.evidence: Section 11 says the listed subsections of Public Law 22-156 shall be amended to read.

provisions.summary: Severability and enactment clause.
provisions.evidence: The act includes severability and says it takes effect immediately after enactment.

current_implementation.detail: The branch flattens multiple repeal, transfer, and amendment actions into one bloated note instead of preserving the specific actions.

recommended_actions: Record the six repeals explicitly.
Add the listed transfer and amendment notes for the District transition provisions.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The District transition act remains operative and governs the repeal, transfer, adoption, and amendment chain for the District-related laws it names.
laws.final_recommended_action: Record the six repeals explicitly. | Add the listed transfer and amendment notes for the District transition provisions. | Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.

laws.exact_change: Repeal the six listed laws in their entirety. | Dissolve the municipal government and transfer MPD, DCFEMS, and the Delegate functions as specified. | Adopt the named municipal laws into federal law with the listed amendments.

laws.title: Public Law 37-261 | Federal Criminal Code Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: The act is operative, but the accessible source exposes only the short title, severability, effective date, and an instruction to adopt an attached Federal Criminal Code whose section-by-section text is not exposed by the source package.

provisions.summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: The source says the bill may be cited as the Federal Criminal Code Act and becomes effective immediately upon enactment.

provisions.summary: Adopts the attached criminal code as the Federal Criminal Code to be enforced in the District of Columbia.
provisions.exact_change: Adopt the attached code as the operative Federal Criminal Code; the accessible source does not expose a section-by-section replacement text.
provisions.evidence: The source says the attached Criminal Code shall act as the Federal Criminal Code.
provisions.notes: This is source-limited because the attached code text is not determinable from the accessible extraction.

current_implementation.detail: The branch should keep this as a source-limited statutory note at the existing anchor; the attached code text cannot be reconstructed from the accessible source package.

recommended_actions: Keep the law as a source-limited statutory note at the existing anchor.
Remove the Trello link.
Do not invent unattested attached-code text.

laws.title: Public Law 38-262 | Fund the Government Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act is temporary appropriations legislation with one repeal of PL 32-230 section 6; it should not become permanent Code text.

provisions.evidence: Fund the Government Act of 2026.

provisions.summary: Definitions for department, appropriations, and emergency fund.
provisions.evidence: The section defines the operative terms for the appropriations measure.

provisions.summary: Budget allocations and emergency fund appropriations.
provisions.evidence: The text says the funds are hereby appropriated.

provisions.summary: Oversight and accountability for fund misuse.
provisions.evidence: The section is oversight and accountability language.

provisions.summary: General Schedule pay scale table.
provisions.evidence: The schedule is general and permanent policy language, not a clean positive-law rewrite target.

provisions.summary: Repeals section 6 of PL 32-230 and assigns schedule authority to OPM.
provisions.exact_change: Section 6 of Public Law 32-230 shall be repealed in its entirety.
provisions.evidence: Section 6 of Public Law 32-230 shall be repealed in its entirety.

provisions.summary: Enactment clause.
provisions.evidence: The bill takes effect immediately after passage.

current_implementation.detail: The branch note captures the appropriations act, but it should stay concise and the appropriations text itself should not be turned into permanent Code text.

recommended_actions: Exclude the appropriations from permanent Code text.
Keep only the repeal note for PL 32-230 section 6.
Trim the dump and Trello material.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The act is temporary appropriations legislation with one repeal of PL 32-230 section 6; it should not become permanent Code text.

laws.title: Public Law 38-263 | National Security Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: This act is the first half of the CRACK Act replacement: it repeals titles I-IV and leaves titles V-VI alive until PL 38-264 finishes the replacement.

provisions.summary: Short title, findings/purposes, definitions, and severability.
provisions.evidence: The opening sections are general statutory-note material.

provisions.summary: Repeals CRACK Act titles I-IV and preserves titles V-VI until this act takes effect.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal Titles I-IV of Public Law 3-21.
provisions.evidence: Titles I through IV of the CRACK Act of 2022 are repealed.

provisions.summary: National Security Council governance and records preservation.
provisions.evidence: The Council provisions are continuing framework language tied to 50 U.S.C. 3021.

provisions.summary: Eligibility determinations, notice, response, review, disclosure limits, and implementation safeguards.
provisions.evidence: The eligibility framework is continuing general law.

provisions.summary: Classification limits and in-camera / ex parte procedure safeguards.
provisions.evidence: The classification and procedure rules are continuing general law.

provisions.summary: Administrative subpoenas and security-sensitive licensing protections.
provisions.evidence: The subpoena and licensing provisions are continuing general law.

current_implementation.detail: The branch note still carries a full-text dump and Trello link; the underlying 50 U.S.C. 3021 framework is already present, so the note should be collapsed to concise history.

recommended_actions: Replace the full-text dump with a concise statutory note at the Title 50 anchor.
Remove the Trello link.
Keep the repeal history for the CRACK Act titles as concise history only.
laws.chronology_conclusion: This act is the first half of the CRACK Act replacement: it repeals titles I-IV and leaves titles V-VI alive until PL 38-264 finishes the replacement.
laws.final_recommended_action: Replace the full-text dump with a concise statutory note at the Title 50 anchor. | Remove the Trello link. | Keep the repeal history for the CRACK Act titles as concise history only.

provisions.summary: Repeals CRACK Act titles V and VI, supplies savings, supersession, and immediate effective date.

provisions.evidence: Titles V and VI of the CRACK Act of 2022 are repealed.

provisions.summary: Prohibited racketeering conduct and attempt/conspiracy rule.
provisions.evidence: The racketeering prohibitions are continuing general law.

provisions.summary: Criminal penalties, forfeiture, and procedural protections.
provisions.evidence: The forfeiture and penalty rules are continuing general law.

provisions.summary: Civil remedies and no private damages action.
provisions.evidence: The civil-remedy limitations are continuing general law.

provisions.summary: Public-trust disqualification rules.
provisions.evidence: The disqualification rules are continuing general law.

provisions.summary: Venue and expedited proceedings.
provisions.evidence: The venue and expedited-handling provisions are continuing general law.

current_implementation.detail: The branch note still carries dump/Trello cleanup, while the underlying RICO framework is already present in the Code.

recommended_actions: Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.
Keep the repeal history and racketeering framework as a concise statutory note unless a precise Title 18 placement is needed.
Mark the CRACK Act title repeal in the prior-law history trail.
laws.chronology_conclusion: This act completes the CRACK Act replacement by repealing titles V-VI after PL 38-263 already repealed titles I-IV.

laws.title: Public Law 38-265 | Employment Clarity and Federal Workforce Modernization Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act remains operative as a broad employment framework and repeals earlier employment statutes without self-expiring.

provisions.summary: Short title, findings/purposes, and definitions.
provisions.evidence: The opening sections are continuing note material.

provisions.summary: Repeals FERA24, the Intelligence Community Employment Act, and the redundant separation framework.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal the named prior laws to the extent stated.
provisions.evidence: The named prior laws are repealed to the extent stated.

provisions.summary: Severability and immediate effective date.
provisions.evidence: The act takes effect immediately and contains severability.

provisions.summary: Private-sector at-will default, discrimination/retaliation limits, and notice rules.
provisions.evidence: The at-will default rule is continuing general law.

provisions.summary: Federal service categories, adverse-action process, provisional service, at-will agreements, emergency suspension, internal review, and judicial review.
provisions.evidence: The federal employment rules are continuing general law.

provisions.summary: Intelligence-community at-will exceptions and security-clearance access limits.
provisions.evidence: The security-clearance and access language is continuing general law.

provisions.summary: Dual-employment and conflict-of-interest restrictions with enforcement.
provisions.evidence: The conflict-of-interest rules are continuing general law.

provisions.summary: Bureau enforcement, private remedies, anti-retaliation, and limitation periods.
provisions.evidence: The enforcement and remedy provisions are continuing general law.

current_implementation.detail: The branch keeps the employment framework as a Title 50 note; it should be collapsed to concise history and the direct amendments should not be left in dump form.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link.
Retain a concise statutory note at a Title 50 employment anchor.
If the repository later codifies this framework, move the direct amendments into actual Title 5 and related-title text.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The act remains operative as a broad employment framework and repeals earlier employment statutes without self-expiring.
laws.final_recommended_action: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link. | Retain a concise statutory note at a Title 50 employment anchor. | If the repository later codifies this framework, move the direct amendments into actual Title 5 and related-title text.
